response.justification: Bogdan Szabo’s resume demonstrates strong software engineering experience, particularly in web/mobile development, embedded systems, and some hardware-adjacent work (e.g., robotic arms, real-time apps). However, his background lacks direct experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep-learning workload analysis, or GPU architecture—key requirements for this Principal Performance Architect role. His expertise in high-level software development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) does not align closely enough with the hardware-centric demands of the position. The absence of advanced degrees or specialized AI/hardware training further weakens his fit.
strengths: Strong software engineering background with experience in embedded systems and hardware-adjacent projects (e.g., robotic arms, real-time apps)
Proven ability to work with performance-critical systems (low-latency apps, caching, high-performance databases)
Open-source contributions and teaching experience, indicating strong technical communication and collaboration skills
concerns: No direct experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, or GPU architecture optimization—core responsibilities of the role
Lacks advanced degrees (PhD/Master’s) in relevant engineering or CS fields, with only a Bachelor’s degree
Background is heavily skewed toward software development (web/mobile/embedded) rather than hardware-software co-design or AI workload optimization
